steps.title: Find the slope of line k using two points from the graph
steps.content:
From the graph, line $k$ passes through $(-6, 1)$ and $(-2, -2)$. Using the slope formula:
$$m_k = \frac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1} = \frac{-2 - 1}{-2 - (-6)} = \frac{-3}{4}$$

steps.title: Apply the perpendicular lines shortcut
steps.content:
Since line $J$ is perpendicular to line $k$, their slopes are negative reciprocals. With $m_k = -\frac{3}{4}$:
$$m_J = -\frac{1}{m_k} = -\frac{1}{-\frac{3}{4}} = \frac{4}{3}$$
Equivalent forms accepted: 4/3, 1.333.
